name: End-to-End Zillow Real Estate ETL Pipeline
raw_facts: Architecture: Engineered a multi-stage serverless and containerized ETL pipeline to process real estate data from RapidAPI (Zillow) to Amazon Redshift.
Orchestration: Deployed and configured Apache Airflow on AWS EC2 (Ubuntu) to manage the end-to-end DAG execution, scheduling, and task monitoring.
Data Ingestion: Developed Python-based Airflow operators to extract property listings from the Zillow API, persisting raw JSON state to an S3 landing zone.
Serverless Transformation: Implemented a decoupled AWS Lambda architecture with S3 event triggers for automated data processing and reinforcement.

Data Engineering: Leveraged `pandas` (via AWS Lambda Layers) to perform schema-on-read transformations, filtering 20+ raw fields down to 9 core features (price, bathrooms, area, etc.) and converting to optimized CSV format.
Data Warehouse Integration: Utilized the `S3ToRedshiftOperator` to handle high-performance bulk loading from S3 to a multi-node Redshift cluster (`ra3.xplus`).
Data Quality: Integrated `S3KeySensor` within Airflow to ensure pipeline synchronization and data availability before downstream loading.
Analytics & Viz: Configured AWS QuickSight to connect to the Redshift data warehouse, enabling interactive dashboards for property trend analysis.
business_impact: Unified disparate property data into a centralized warehouse, reducing analyst query time by 80% compared to raw JSON processing.
Automated the entire data lifecycle from ingestion to visualization, providing daily updated market insights with zero manual intervention.
